low.outdoor_activities: Safe for all outdoor activities. Ensure normal hydration.

moderate.outdoor_activities: Safe, but take breaks in the shade. Drink water hourly.
moderate.peak_hour_restrictions: Limit high-intensity outdoor activities (e.g. running, sports) between 12:00 PM and 3:00 PM.
moderate.indoor_activities: Ensure proper ventilation or fan use.
moderate.work_recommendations: Ensure outdoor workers have access to shade and clean water. Take 5-minute rest breaks every hour.

high.outdoor_activities: Restrict high-intensity exercise. Wear sun protection. Walk in shaded paths.
high.peak_hour_restrictions: Avoid direct sun exposure and strenuous outdoor tasks between 11:00 AM and 4:00 PM.
high.indoor_activities: Use air conditioning if available, or visit public cooling centers. Avoid cooking during peak hours if kitchen is hot.
high.work_recommendations: Implement work-rest cycles (e.g., 15 minutes of rest for every 45 minutes of work). Shift heavy labor to early morning (before 10:00 AM) or late evening (after 5:00 PM).

extreme.outdoor_activities: Cancel all non-essential outdoor activities. Strenuous exercise is highly dangerous.
extreme.peak_hour_restrictions: Do not go outdoors between 10:00 AM and 6:00 PM unless absolutely necessary.
extreme.indoor_activities: Stay in cool, air-conditioned rooms. If indoor temperature exceeds 35°C (95°F), fans alone may not prevent heat illness; seek air-conditioned shelter.
extreme.work_recommendations: Suspend outdoor physical labor. Move tasks indoors to cooled areas, or reschedule. Critical outdoor work must follow a strict 30-minute work, 30-minute rest cycle in shaded/cooled zones with mandatory hydration.

acclimatization.definition: The process of gradually adjusting to hot environments over 7 to 14 days to reduce heat stress risk.
acclimatization.new_workers: New workers should work at 20% intensity on Day 1, with a 20% daily increase in duration and intensity, reaching full capacity by Day 5 or 6.
acclimatization.returning_workers: Workers returning after an absence should start at 50% intensity on Day 1, 60% on Day 2, 80% on Day 3, and 100% on Day 4.

heat_exhaustion.action: Move to a cool place. Loosen clothing. Apply cool, wet cloths or take a cool bath. Sip water slowly. Seek medical attention if symptoms worsen or last more than 1 hour.

symptoms: Hot, red, dry or damp skin
heat_stroke.action: This is a medical emergency. Call emergency services (e.g., 108/102 in India) immediately. Move the person to a cool place. Cool the person using cool cloths, ice packs, or a cool bath. Do NOT give them anything to drink.
